question.text: A trader sells 10 lots of USDINR futures at 84.20. At expiry, the FBIL reference rate is 83.80. What is the profit/loss?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Short position profits when price falls. P&L = (Entry - Final) × Lot × Lots = (84.20 - 83.80) × 1000 × 10 = 0.40 × 10000 = Rs. 4,000 profit. **The Trap:** For short positions, profit = entry price MINUS settlement price (not the other way around). **Cross-Exam Context:** Short futures P&L is tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The final settlement price is the FBIL reference rate, not the last traded futures price.
